action.name: Tentacles
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 8}, reach 5 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) Psychic damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Necrotic damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 14}) from all the wight's tentacles, and the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the grapple ends.

action.name: Extract Brain
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 16}, one creature that is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the wight's Tentacles. {@actSaveFail} 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Necrotic damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} If this damage reduces the target to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the wight kills it and devours its brain.

action.name: Life Drain
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 15}, one creature within 5 feet. {@actSaveFail} 16 ({@damage 3d8 + 3}) Necrotic damage, and the target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum decreases by an amount equal to the damage taken. A Humanoid slain by this attack rises 24 hours later as a Zombie under the wight's control, unless the Humanoid is restored to life or its body is destroyed. The wight can have no more than twelve zombies under its control at a time.

action.name: Mind Blast {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave int} {@dc 16}, each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 31 ({@damage 6d8 + 4}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of the wight's next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

trait.name: Aura of Hope and Despair
entries: The wisp emanates an aura in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} while it doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. {@actSave wis} {@dc 15}, each creature of the wisp's choice in the aura at the start of the wisp's turn. {@actSaveFail} The creature has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition until the start of the wisp's next turn. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, the creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} of Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks and must use its movement on its turns to get as close as possible to the target, moving by the safest route. If the creature is within 5 feet of the target, the creature can't willingly move away. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to this wisp's aura for 24 hours.

trait.name: Ephemeral
entries: The wisp can't wear or carry anything.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The wisp sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The wisp can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wisp makes two Shock attacks.

action.name: Shock
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d8 + 4}) Lightning damage.

bonus.name: Consume Life
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 12}, one living creature the wisp can see within 5 feet that has 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. {@actSaveFail} The target dies, and the wisp regains 17 ({@dice 5d6}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

bonus.name: Vanish
entries: The wisp and its light have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until the wisp's {@status Concentration|XPHB} ends on this effect, which ends early immediately after the wisp makes an attack roll or uses Consume Life.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The wolf has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the wolf's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d6 + 6}) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Cold Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 15}, each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Cold damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

bonus.name: Primal Howl {@recharge}
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 15}, each enemy that can hear the wolf in a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the wolf. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of the wolf's next turn. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} Up to two allies that can hear the wolf can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} or make one melee attack.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The dread wraith casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Aura of Dread
entries: Creatures in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the dread wraith have their Speeds halved while in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. The dread wraith can designate creatures to be unaffected by the aura. Creatures immune to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition are unaffected.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The dread wraith can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the dread wraith has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ability checks and attack rolls.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dread wraith makes two Life Drain attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting to cast {@spell Bestow Curse|XPHB}, {@spell Darkness|XPHB}, or {@spell Ray of Enfeeblement|XPHB}.

action.name: Life Drain
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 8}, reach 5 ft. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d8 + 4}) Necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum decreases by an amount equal to the damage taken.

action.name: Create Wraith
entries: The dread wraith targets a Humanoid corpse within 10 feet of itself that has been dead for no longer than 1 minute. The target's spirit rises as a Wraith in the space of its corpse or in the nearest unoccupied space. The wraith is under the dread wraith's control. The dread wraith can have no more than three wraiths under its control at a time.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The dread wraith casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 19}):

trait.name: Aura of Dread
entries: Creatures in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the dread wraith have their Speeds halved while in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. The dread wraith can designate creatures to be unaffected by the aura. Creatures immune to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition are unaffected.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The dread wraith can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the dread wraith fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the dread wraith has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ability checks and attack rolls.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dread wraith makes two attacks, using Life Draining Sword or Necrotic Bolt in any combination. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

action.name: Life Draining Sword
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 11}, reach 5 ft. {@h}14 ({@dice 2d8 + 5}) plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum decreases by an amount equal to the damage taken.

action.name: Necrotic Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 11}, range 120 ft. {@h}23 ({@damage 4d8 + 5}) Necrotic damage and the target can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Create Wraith
entries: The dread wraith targets a Humanoid corpse within 10 feet of itself that has been dead for no longer than 1 minute. The target's spirit rises as a Wraith in the space of its corpse or in the nearest unoccupied space. The wraith is under the dread wraith's control. The dread wraith can have no more than six wraiths under its control at a time.

legendary.name: Command Undead
entries: One undead ally that can see or hear the dread wraith can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and make one attack.

legendary.name: Dark Bolt
entries: The dread wraith makes one Necrotic Bolt attack.

legendary.name: Deathless Strike
entries: The dread wraith moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and it makes one Life Draining Sword attack.

legendary.name: Dreadful Magic
entries: The dread wraith uses Spellcasting to cast {@spell Bestow Curse|XPHB}, {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB}, {@spell Fear|XPHB}, or {@spell Ray of Enfeeblement|XPHB}. The dread wraith can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

legendary.name: Teleport
entries: The dread wraith teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space the dread wraith can see. The dread wraith can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the wyvern fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The wyvern makes one Bite attack, one Talons attack, and one Sting attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 10 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Talons
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 5 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d4 + 6}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Sting
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 15 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Piercing damage plus 24 ({@damage 7d6}) Poison damage, and the target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the start of the wyvern's next turn.

legendary.name: Flyby
entries: The wyvern moves up to half its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

legendary.name: Sting
entries: The wyvern makes one Sting attack. The wyvern can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

legendary.name: Swoop
entries: The wyvern moves up to half its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}, and it makes one Talons attack.

trait.name: Earth Glide
entries: The xorn can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone. While doing so, the xorn doesn't disturb the material it moves through.

trait.name: Treasure Frenzy
entries: The xorn has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against any creature is carrying precious metals and stones.

trait.name: Treasure Sense
entries: The xorn can pinpoint the location of precious metals and stones within 60 feet of itself.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The xorn makes one Bite attack and three Claw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. {@h}26 ({@damage 6d6 + 5}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) Slashing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}) from one of three claws.

bonus.name: Charge
entries: The xorn moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} or {@variantrule Burrow Speed|XPHB} straight toward an enemy it can sense.

trait.name: Alpha's Presence
entries: Allies of the yeti's choice (excluding itself) in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and saving throws. It can't use this trait if it is {@status Bloodied|XPHB} or has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} or {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Fear of Fire
entries: If the yeti takes Fire damage, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If the yeti fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The yeti can use its Chilling Gaze and makes two attacks, using Claw or Ice Throw in any combination.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 12}, reach 5 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d6 + 7}) Slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Cold damage.

action.name: Ice Throw
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 12}, range 60/240 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d4 + 7}) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Cold damage.

action.name: Chilling Gaze
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 19}, one creature the yeti can see within 30 feet. {@actSaveFail} 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Cold damage, and the target has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition until the start of the yeti's next turn unless the target has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Cold damage. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to this yeti's Chilling Gaze for 1 hour.

action.name: Cold Breath {@recharge}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 19}, each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 54 ({@damage 12d8}) Cold damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

legendary.name: Freeze
entries: The yeti makes one Chilling Gaze attack. The yeti can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

legendary.name: Mauling Pounce
entries: The yeti moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and it makes one Claw attack.

legendary.name: Pelt
entries: The yeti makes one Ice Throw attack.

trait.name: Fear of Fire
entries: If the yeti takes Fire damage, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Ice Throw

action.name: Chilling Gaze

trait.name: Fear of Fire
entries: If the yeti takes Fire damage, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Snow Strider

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The yeti can use its Chilling Gaze and makes two attacks, using Claw or Ice Throw in any combination.

action.name: Ice Throw
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 6}, range 30/120 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d4 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Cold damage, plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Cold damage if the attack roll had {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

action.name: Chilling Gaze
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 13}, one creature the yeti can see within 30 feet. {@actSaveFail} 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Cold damage, and the target has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition until the start of the yeti's next turn unless the target has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Cold damage. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to the Chilling Gaze of all yetis (but not abominable yetis) for 1 hour.

bonus.name: Stealthy
entries: The yeti takes the {@action Hide|XPHB} action.

It can't use this trait if it has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The yuan-ti makes two attacks, using Bite or Longsword in any combination. It also uses Spellcasting to cast {@spell Charm Person|XPHB}, {@spell Darkness|XPHB}, {@spell Ray of Sickness|XPHB} (level 2 version) or {@spell Suggestion|XPHB} if available.

action.name: Longsword

The target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 14}), and it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the grapple ends. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

action.name: Poison Spray {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 16}, each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Poison damage, and the target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of the yuan-ti's next turn. While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

bonus.name: Shape-Shift
entries: The yuan-ti shape-shifts into a Large snake or returns to its true form. If it dies, it stays in its current form. The yuan-ti's game statistics are the same in each form, except where noted. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

legendary.name: Exert Will
entries: One creature {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by the yuan-ti that can see or hear the yuan-ti takes a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and make one attack against a creature chosen by the yuan-ti.

legendary.name: Sinister Magic
entries: The yuan-ti uses Spellcasting to cast Charm

legendary.name: Person, Darkness, Fear, or Ray of Sickness (level 2 version)
entries: The yuan-ti can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

legendary.name: Slithering Strike
entries: The yuan-ti moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and it makes one Bite attack.

While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way, the creature takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Poison damage after every 8 hours. When it dies, the creature is reanimated as a Spore Zombie.

entries: The bear makes three Rend attacks.

entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) Slashing damage.

entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 16}, each enemy in a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the bear. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of the bear's next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to this bear's Intimidating Roar for 24 hours.

entries: The rat has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the rat's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entries: The ant has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the ant's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The ant can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 feet. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d6 + 1}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Sting
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 feet. {@h}2 Piercing damage, and the target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of the ant's next turn.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The bug can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Proboscis
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) Piercing damage, the bug attaches to the target, and the target is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 11}. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition. While attached, the bug can't make Proboscis attacks, and the target takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Necrotic damage at the start of each of the bug's turns. The bug can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. The target or a creature within 5 feet of it can detach the bug with an action.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The bullfrog can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The bullfrog's {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} is up to 30 feet and its {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} is up to 15 feet with or without a running start.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 13}).

action.name: Swallow
entries: The bullfrog swallows a Medium or smaller target it is grappling. While swallowed, the target isn't {@condition Grappled|XPHB} but has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, and it has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against attacks and other effects outside the bullfrog. While swallowing the target, the bullfrog can't use Bite or Tongue, and if the bullfrog dies, the swallowed target is no longer {@condition Restrained|XPHB} and can escape from the corpse using 5 feet of movement, exiting with the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

At the end of the bullfrog's next turn, the swallowed target takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Acid damage. If that damage doesn't kill it, the bullfrog disgorges it, causing it to exit {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

bonus.name: Tongue
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 15 ft. {@h}If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, the bullfrog pulls the target 10 feet straight toward itself.

trait.name: Amphibious

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d6 + 3}) Piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Poison damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 13}).

bonus.name: Swallow
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 13}, one Medium or smaller creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the caecilia (it can have up to two creatures swallowed at a time). {@actSaveFail} The target is swallowed by the caecilia, and the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition ends. A swallowed creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against attacks and other effects outside the caecilia, and takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Acid damage at the start of each of the caecilia's turns.

If the caecilia takes 10 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the caecilia must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, each of which falls in a space within 5 feet of the caecilia and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. If the caecilia dies, any swallowed creature no longer has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition and can escape from the corpse using 5 feet of movement, exiting {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

The leech can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement.

11. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.
While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition.

While attached, the fly can't make Proboscis attacks, and the target takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Necrotic damage at the start of each of the fly's turns. The fly can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. The target or a creature within 5 feet of it can detach the fly with an action.

It then starts a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, at the end of which that condition ends on it. The condition ends early if the creature regains any {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or if someone takes an action to administer first aid to it, making a successful {@dc 10} Wisdom ({@skill Medicine|XPHB}) check.

The {@condition Charmed|XPHB} target must use its action before moving on each of its turns to make a melee attack against a creature other than itself that the whisperer mentally chooses. The target can act normally on its turn if the whisperer chooses no creature or if no creature is within its reach. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to this whisperer's Dark Whispers for 24 hours.

entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 15}, each creature in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on a point the priest can see within 60 feet. {@actSaveFail} 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Necrotic damage and the target can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until the end of its next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

headerEntries: The divine trickster casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 14}):

spellcasting.name: Invoke Duplicity (2/Day)
headerEntries: The divine trickster casts {@spell Project Image|XPHB}, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The divine trickster makes two attacks, using Mace or Befuddling Bolt in any combination.

action.name: Mace
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Psychic damage, and the target can't take Reactions until the start of the divine trickster's next turn.

action.name: Befuddling Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 7}, range 90 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) Psychic damage, and the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws until the end of the divine trickster's next turn.

reaction.name: Misty Retreat (3/Day)
entries: {@actTrigger} The divine trickster takes damage. {@actResponse} The divine trickster casts {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The dread lord casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}):

spellcasting.name: Dreadful Word (1/Day)
headerEntries: The dread lord casts {@spell Divine Word|XPHB}, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

trait.name: Aura of Dread
entries: Creatures in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the dread lord have their Speeds halved while in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. The dread lord can designate creatures to be unaffected by the aura. Creatures immune to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition are unaffected.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the dread lord fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dread lord makes two attacks, using Dreadful Morningstar or Dreadful Bolt in any combination.

action.name: Dreadful Morningstar
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9} (with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} if the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition), reach 5 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of the dread lord's next turn.

action.name: Dreadful Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 10} (with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} if the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition), range 120 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d8}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of the dread lord's next turn.

legendary.name: Dreadful Bolt
entries: The dread lord makes one Dreadful Bolt attack.

legendary.name: Dreadful Charge
entries: The dread lord moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and it makes one Dreadful Morningstar attack.

legendary.name: Dreadful Magic
entries: The dread lord uses Spellcasting to cast {@spell Command|XPHB} (level 3 version), {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB}, {@spell Fear|XPHB}, and {@spell Hold Person|XPHB} (level 3 version). The dread lord can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The dreadmaster casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 16}):

spellcasting.name: Smite (3/Day)
headerEntries: The dreadmaster casts {@spell Blinding Smite|XPHB} or {@spell Staggering Smite|XPHB}, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

trait.name: Aura of Dread
entries: Creatures in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the dreadmaster have their Speeds halved while in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. The dreadmaster can designate creatures to be unaffected by the aura. Creatures immune to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition are unaffected.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dreadmaster makes three attacks, using Dire Longsword or Dire Bolt in any combination. It can replace one attack with a use of Commander's Strike or Spellcasting to cast {@spell Bane|XPHB}, {@spell Command|XPHB}, or {@spell Hold Person|XPHB}.

action.name: Dire Longsword
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Psychic damage, and the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom saving throws until the end of the dreadmaster's next turn.

action.name: Dire Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 8}, range 120 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d8}) Psychic damage, and the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom saving throws until the end of the dreadmaster's next turn.

action.name: Commander's Strike
entries: One ally that can see or hear the dreadmaster can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} at a target the dreadmaster chooses, adding 9 ({@dice 2d8}) to the attack's damage roll on a hit.

bonus.name: Dreadmaster's Presence {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 16}, each enemy in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the dreadmaster. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} and {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition until the end of the dreadmaster's next turn.

spellcasting.name: Hunter's Mark (2/Day)
headerEntries: The foe hunter casts {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, requiring no spell components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The foe hunter makes two attacks, using Longsword or Longbow in any combination.

action.name: Longsword
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Slashing damage, or 12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) Slashing damage if the target doesn't have all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 5}, range 150/600 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Piercing damage, or 12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) Piercing damage if the target doesn't have all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The priest casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

spellcasting.name: Auril's Aid (3/Day)
headerEntries: The priest casts {@spell Armor of Agathys|XPHB}, {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB}, {@spell Healing Word|XPHB}, or {@spell Sleet Storm|XPHB}, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The priest makes two attacks, using Frost Pick or Frost Ray in any combination.

action.name: Frost Pick
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Piercing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Cold damage.

action.name: Frost Ray
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 7}, range 120 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d8}) Cold damage.

action.name: Winter's Bloom {@recharge 5}
entries: The priest conjures an explosion of freezing, ice shards. {@actSave con} {@dc 15}, each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point the priest can see within 60 feet. {@actSaveFail} 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Cold damage, and the target is partially encased in ice. The target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the ice encasing it is destroyed (AC 10; HP 20; {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting

spellcasting.name: Shield (2/Day)
headerEntries: The hexblade casts {@spell Shield|XPHB} in response to the spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) Slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Hex Bolt
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 6}, range 120 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) Psychic damage and the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@dice D20} Tests until the end of the hexblade's next turn.

bonus.name: Hexblade's Curse {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 14}, One creature the hexblade can see within 30 feet. {@actSaveFail} The target is cursed for 1 minute. Until the curse ends, the target is Vulnerable to the hexblade's melee attack damage.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting

headerEntries: The hex knight casts {@spell Shield|XPHB} in response to the spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) Slashing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Psychic damage.

entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 9}, range 120 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d8 + 4}) Psychic damage and the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@dice D20} Tests until the end of the hex knight's next turn.

entries: The hex knight moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and it makes one Scimitar attack.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The inquisitor casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}):

headerEntries: The inquisitor casts {@spell Banishing Smite|XPHB}, {@spell Blinding Smite|XPHB} (level 4 version), {@spell Staggering Smite|XPHB}, or {@spell Wrathful Smite|XPHB} (level 4 version), using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

entries: If inquisitor fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Dire Flail
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 5 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d8 + 5}) Bludgeoning damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Psychic damage, and the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom saving throws until the end of the inquisitor's next turn.

action.name: Glare of Reckoning
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 17}, one creature the inquisitor can see within 30 feet. {@actSaveFail} 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of the inquisitor's next turn. While {@condition Frightened|XPHB} in this way, the creature has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

legendary.name: Charge
entries: The inquisitor moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and it makes one Dire Flail attack.

legendary.name: Castigating Magic
entries: The inquisitor uses Spellcasting to cast {@spell Command|XPHB} (level 2 version), {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB}, {@spell Fear|XPHB}, or {@spell Hold Person|XPHB}.
The inquisitor can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

entries: Allies of the marshal's choice (excluding itself) in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls and saving throws. It can't use this trait if it has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entries: The marshal makes three attacks, using Longsword or Light Crossbow in any combination. It can replace one attack with a use of Commander's Strike or Maneuver Allies, if available.

action.name: Light Crossbow
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 5}, range 80/320 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Commander's Strike
entries: One ally that can see or hear the marshal can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} at a target the marshal chooses, adding 9 ({@dice 2d8}) to the attack's damage roll on a hit.

action.name: Maneuver Allies {@recharge 5}
entries: Each ally within 30 feet of the marshal can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

bonus.name: Rallying Call (1/Day)
entries: Up to three allies of the marshal that can see or hear it gain 10 ({@dice 1d10 + 5}) {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

action.name: Longsword
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Light Crossbow
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 2}, range 80/320 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d8}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Commander's Strike
entries: One ally that can see or hear the sergeant can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} at a target the sergeant chooses, adding 4 ({@dice 1d8}) to the attack's damage roll on a hit.

action.name: Maneuver Allies {@recharge 5}
entries: Each ally within 30 feet of the sergeant can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

entries: If the shadow fist is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, the shadow fist instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the save and only half damage if it fails. It can't use this trait if it has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 13}, one creature within 5 feet. {@actSaveFail} 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Necrotic damage plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the start of the shadow fist's next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

entries: The shadow fist takes the {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, or {@action Hide|XPHB} action.

1/Day Each: {@spell Call Lightning|XPHB}, {@spell Ice Storm|XPHB}, {@spell Shatter|XPHB}

action.name: Cyclone Blast (1/Day)
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 14}, each creature and each object that isn't being worn or carried in a 30-foot-long, 10-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}. {@actSaveFail} 22 ({@damage 5d8}) Thunder damage, and if the target is a Large or smaller creature, the target is pushed up to 30 feet straight away from the shaman and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The sorcerer casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

spellcasting.name: Protective Magic (2/Day)
headerEntries: The sorcerer casts {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} or {@spell Shield|XPHB} in response to the spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

trait.name: Heart of the Storm
entries: When the sorcerer casts {@spell Cacophonic Shield|FRHoF}, {@spell Lightning Bolt|XPHB}, {@spell Shatter|XPHB}, or {@spell Thunderwave|XPHB}, all other creatures within 10 feet of the sorcerer each take 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Lightning damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The sorcerer makes two Shock Burst attacks.

action.name: Shock Burst
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft. or range 90 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d10}) Lightning damage and the target can't take Reactions until the end of the sorcerer's next turn.

action.name: Storm Fall {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 15}, each creature in a 20-foot-radius, 40-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} originating from a point the sorcerer can see within 500 feet. {@actSaveFail} 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Lightning damage and 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Thunder damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

bonus.name: Quickened Spell (2/Day)
entries: The sorcerer uses Spellcasting.

monsterFluff.name: Aarakocra Talon of Syranita
entries: Once in a generation, a special aarakocra is born under a divine omen and chosen by the avian goddess Syranita. This blessed champion becomes a living extension of the deity’s will on the mortal plane. Guided by the tribe's elders, they are rigorously trained to serve as both spiritual leaders and fierce guardians. Known as a Talon of Syranita, this celestial warrior wields divine magic to smite its enemies. They channel the tempestuous power of biting cold, lightning, and thunder to protect their sacred roosts. Clad in radiant feathers that shimmer with ethereal wind currents, they embody the perfect harmony between unwavering faith and the raging storm. Aarakocras Brave Talon of Syranita Scout

monsterFluff.name: Aboleth Overseer
entries: One of the most powerful and exceedingly insidious creatures found in the Realms, the overseer is twice the size of a normal aboleth and is an ancient being that has enslaved countless cities and civilizations, leaving despair in its wake. When an aboleth overseer sets its eyes on a new city to conquer, it begins by enslaving locals who live near the coasts and other bodies of water that the aboleth has access to. Once dominated by the aboleth, the enslaved minions are sent to gather information and bring back other victims to enslave. The aboleth overseer does all of these while being careful not to reveal its true appearance. The aboleth overseer then works its way up the social ladder, using the enslaved locals to return with strategic targets, such as city officials, wealthy merchants, etc. Once it has established a network of controlled individuals in strategic positions, it strengthens its stranglehold of the city through bribery, coercion, murder, and enslavement. Within a few years, the aboleth overseer may have total control of an entire city, serving as its secret tyrant. When the aboleth overseer is satisfied that it has fully divested a city's wealth and knowledge, it instructs its victims to sow distrust and cause mayhem, resulting in civil unrest, chaos, and mass killings. It is so devious in its methods that it may leave a city devastated without anyone knowing of its true nature. An Aboleth Overseer's Lair Aboleth overseers lair in subterranean lakes or the rocky depths of the ocean, often within a mile of a large town or city, and often surrounded by the hidden ruins of an ancient, fallen city. An aboleth overseer's lair is always guarded by dozens of skum, as well as other water-breathing races that the aboleth has enslaved. The region containing an aboleth overseer’s lair is warped by the creature’s presence, which creates one or more of the following effects: Putrid Water. Water sources within 5 miles of the lair are supernaturally fouled. Creatures other than the aboleth and its allies

that drink such water must succeed on a DC 21 Constitution saving throw or have the Poisoned condition for 12 hours. While Poisoned in this way, the creature has Disadvantage to D20 Tests. Psionic Projection. While in its lair, the aboleth can cast Project Image, requiring no spell components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 21). When casting the spell this way, the spell’s range is 5 miles, and the aboleth can use its telepathy as if it were in the illusion’s space. Slimy Surfaces. Underground surfaces within 5 mile of the lair are slimy and wet, and they are Difficult Terrain. If the aboleth overseer dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monsterFluff.name: Aboleth Slime Lord
entries: Drifting from the abyssal depths and the unfathomable Far Realm, aboleth slime lords are bloated aberrations that thrive in stagnant subterranean lakes. These foul entities seek out festering environments where rot and pollution spread unchecked to establish their corrupted domains. Their elongated bodies are visibly swollen with mucous, featuring a partially liquefied hide that reveals pulsating internal organs through semi-translucent membranes. As they glide through darkened waters or drag themselves across the mud, their heavy limbs constantly weep an extremely adhesive filth. Deeply possessive of their aquatic territories, aboleth slime lords view all other beings as mere raw material to be mentally subjugated or gradually dissolved by their cursed aura. They masterfully hurl globes of caustic sludge to trap adversaries before closing the distance to extract precious memories from broken minds. Loyal thralls of the Abolethic Sovereignty intentionally release these mucilaginous horrors to contaminate the sprawling subterranean waters of the Glimmersea. Aquatic elven scouts frequently report sightings of these creatures within the deepest trenches of the Sea of Fallen Stars, where they reshape entire coral reefs into polluted strongholds. Furthermore, intrepid rangers patrolling the rotting bogs of the Flooded Forest often discover these beings transforming the stagnant surface swamplands into highly toxic breeding grounds.

monsterFluff.name: Shaytan Solar
entries: Serving as the final safeguard for a ruling demon prince, this exceptionally scarce fallen solar acts as a supreme bodyguard and general. Because their staggering might rivals that of their masters, these brooding warlords are kept under constant surveillance to prevent them from staging a sudden abyssal coup. When deployed to the mortal sphere, the shaytan solars are usually found unearthing forbidden binding rites in the sweeping steppes of Nar Fell or securing lost relics beneath the ruined capital of Shoonach. They hover above the ground on obsidian wings, wielding an impossibly huge bow that promises immediate death to any creature lacking extraordinary vitality. Upon engaging an interfering vanguard, the fallen solar instantly projects a paralyzing gaze of pure terror to halt approaching warriors in their tracks. It then gracefully teleports across the arena, raining down flesh-rotting arrows and commanding an autonomous flying blade to systematically execute outflanked adversaries.

monsterFluff.name: Aarakocra Brave
entries: Aarakocra braves are the fiercest and largest warriors among their avian kind. They are absolute masters of aerial combat who strike from above with deadly precision. Employing devastating hit-and-run tactics, these elite fighters utilize their natural agility to swoop past enemy defenses unscathed. During a skirmish, they do not hesitate to target the most dangerous foes to draw attention away from vulnerable allies. To aid in these coordinated aerial strikes, they carry light shields to deflect incoming attacks. They also wield magical storm spears imbued with the destructive power of lightning and thundering attacks while circling the battlefield in coordinated aerial strikes.

monsterFluff.name: Aarakocra Scout
entries: Aarakocra scouts serve as the dedicated hunters and vigilant sentries of their high-altitude roosts. They constantly patrol the open skies to conduct long-range reconnaissance missions. While soaring through the clouds, they keep a watchful eye for any signs of danger or opportunity in the surrounding lands. These quick and elusive flyers usually attempt to discourage intruders with warning volleys of javelins. They only resort to lethal force when absolutely necessary to protect their remote homes. Highly skilled at evasive maneuvers, an aarakocra scout can effortlessly disengage from enemies to warn their tribe of impending threats.

monsterFluff.name: Skum
entries: Skum represent the tragic remnants of surface-dwellers who fell victim to the psychic enslavement and mutagenic slime of aboleth masters. These amphibious thralls have been stripped of their former identities, reshaped into loyal servants that patrol the lightless depths of sunken cities. Their skin is perpetually coated in a translucent, foul-smelling mucus that allows them to thrive in the crushing pressures of the lightless deep. The common skum variety appears as a hunched, web-footed humanoid that serves in vast numbers as common laborers or expendable fodder. In contrast, the skum beast variant originates from twisted predatory animals, utilizing heightened olfactory and auditory senses to track down any who attempt to flee the master's domain. Massive specimens known as hulks are forged from the frames of ogres or large bipeds to act as formidable personal protectors. The most terrifying form is the skum giant, a rare champion born from a fully realized behemoth such as a cyclops or hill giant that possesses the sheer physical power to crush any opposition to the aboleth's grand designs.

monsterFluff.name: Skum Hulk
entries: Skum represent the tragic remnants of surface-dwellers who fell victim to the psychic enslavement and mutagenic slime of aboleth masters. These amphibious thralls have been stripped of their former identities, reshaped into loyal servants that patrol the lightless depths of sunken cities. entries: Skum represent the tragic remnants of surface-dwellers who fell victim to the psychic enslavement and mutagenic slime of aboleth masters. monsterFluff.name: Animated Iron Statue
entries: Animated iron statues are forged from blackened or rust-hued metal and polished to appear as ornamental suits of armor, heroic effigies, or decorative sentinels. They are most often installed in fortress halls, throne rooms, or vault chambers, where their metallic sheen and imposing stance add to the decor’s grandeur. Their seamless joints and rigid posture make them indistinguishable from normal iron statues until they spring to life, weapons clanging as they defend their warded domain.

monsterFluff.name: Large Animated Iron Statue
entries: Animated iron statues are forged from blackened or rust-hued metal and polished to appear as ornamental suits of armor, heroic effigies, or decorative sentinels. They are most often installed in fortress halls, throne rooms, or vault chambers, where their metallic sheen and imposing stance add to the decor’s grandeur. Their seamless joints and rigid posture make them indistinguishable from normal iron statues until they spring to life, weapons clanging as they defend their warded domain.

monsterFluff.name: Shaytan Deva
entries: These fallen emissaries now act as abyssal spies, utilizing their shape- altering capabilities to travel undetected across the mortal realm and its echoing planar reflections. Tasked with seeding discord on behalf of demonic masters, they frequently infiltrate Westgate's illicit syndicates or manipulate rival gangs within the fog-choked streets of Luskan. Bereft of their heavenly glow, these defiled, feathered entities brandish bludgeons infused with entropic power. They loathe righteousness and delight in arranging complex treacheries that gradually shatter the resolve of their targets. When confronted by a righteous party, the treacherous spy immediately cloaks itself in shadow to launch crippling hexes and plagues from the periphery of the battlefield. It expertly weaves through the gloom to bash vulnerable spellcasters with its tainted weapon, relying on its innate resilience to shrug off retaliatory enchantments.

monsterFluff.name: Shaytan Planetar
entries: Recognized for their tactical brilliance and sheer brawn, these disgraced champions now serve as frontline commanders and elite sentinels for the most ambitious demon princes. They frequently spearhead abyssal invasions into the sunless depths of Menzoberranzan or dutifully guard active planar rifts hidden across the freezing wastes of Vaasa. Their commanding voices echo with abyssal authority, ensuring their anarchic battalions remain focused on total annihilation. Dispatched specifically to annihilate meddling heroes, a shaytan planetar initiates combat by detonating a sprawling radius of flesh-withering energy to scatter tight formations. It then plunges into the fray with a blighted greatsword, effortlessly rendering itself unseen to execute lethal ambushes while easily ignoring the strongest counter-magic.

monsterFluff.name: Animated Stone Statue
entries: Animated stone statues are hewn from marble, granite, or limestone and shaped into figures of kings, warriors, angels, or divine protectors. They are typically placed in crypts, temple corridors, palace entryways, or mausoleums, blending naturally among genuine memorial sculptures. Their weathered appearance and finely chiseled details help them remain unnoticed, motionless for years until an intruder triggers their hidden animation.

monsterFluff.name: Animated Wood Statue
entries: Animated wood statues are carved from sturdy hardwoods such as oak, teak, or mahogany, and often lacquered or painted to resemble fine decorative sculptures. They are commonly found in noble estates, garden courtyards, and temples where wood is abundant, serving as inconspicuous sentinels among other carvings or furnishings. Their designs frequently depict serene monks, mythical beasts, or ornate deities, crafted with such artistry that few suspect they conceal an enchantment until the statue suddenly moves.

monsterFluff.name: Large Animated Stone Statue
entries: Animated stone statues are hewn from marble, granite, or limestone and shaped into figures of kings, warriors, angels, or divine protectors. They are typically placed in crypts, temple corridors, palace entryways, or mausoleums, blending naturally among genuine memorial sculptures. Their weathered appearance and finely chiseled details help them remain unnoticed, motionless for years until an intruder triggers their hidden animation.

monsterFluff.name: Large Animated Wood Statue
entries: Animated wood statues are carved from sturdy hardwoods such as oak, teak, or mahogany, and often lacquered or painted to resemble fine decorative sculptures. They are commonly found in noble estates, garden courtyards, and temples where wood is abundant, serving as inconspicuous sentinels among other carvings or furnishings. Their designs frequently depict serene monks, mythical beasts, or ornate deities, crafted with such artistry that few suspect they conceal an enchantment until the statue suddenly moves.

monsterFluff.name: Ankheg Broodling
entries: Ankheg soldiers are larger, stronger, and more resilient than typical ankhegs, with broader heads and massive mandibles built for crushing prey. They serve as the primary defenders of the brood queen’s lair, responding swiftly to any disturbance within the tunnels. Relentless in battle, they fight to the death and drag fallen enemies back to the nest to feed the colony.

monsterFluff.name: Ankheg Brood Queen
entries: An ankheg brood queen is rarely seen, spending nearly all its life deep underground where it is guarded by its brood and layers of soil and stone. It is nearly three times the size of a typical ankheg, with a massive, distended abdomen swollen with egg sacs. The brood queen rules a vast network of tunnels that may extend for miles. It ruthlessly hunts down rival queens, even its own offspring, to eliminate any threat to its dominion. When ankheg attacks display uncanny coordination or discipline, it is often the result of a brood queen’s influence. Adventurers may be hired to root out such a lair, a perilous task that leads into a labyrinth of tunnels swarming with soldiers and drones. Brood queens frequently carve out expansive hive networks beneath the fertile soil of the Dessarin Valley. They are equally notorious for establishing immense subterranean colonies beneath the sweeping grasslands of the Shaar, terrorizing the nomadic tribes that traverse the open plains.

monsterFluff.name: Ankheg Soldier
entries: Ankheg soldiers are larger, stronger, and more resilient than typical ankhegs, with broader heads and massive mandibles built for crushing prey. They serve as the primary defenders of the brood queen’s lair, responding swiftly to any disturbance within the tunnels. Relentless in battle, they fight to the death and drag fallen enemies back to the nest to feed the colony.

monsterFluff.name: Azer Commoner
entries: Azer commoners are typically laborers, soldiers, and smiths who tirelessly shape metal and stone in service to their citadels and kings on the Elemental Plane of Fire. Keeper of the Flame Azer Keepers of the Flame are high priests of Kossuth who tend sacred forges and eternal fires within the blazing citadels of the Plane of Fire. Their molten bronze bodies radiate divine heat as they lead rituals of purification, forging weapons and faith alike in the name of the Firelord. In battle, they wield both hammer and holy flame, calling upon Kossuth’s power to sear the unworthy and rekindle the strength of their allies. Taskmaster Ruthless overseers of labor and battle, Azer Taskmasters rule through fear and absolute discipline. They drive slaves and subordinates with relentless cruelty, viewing them as expendable tools in the grand workings of the forge or the warfront. In combat, a taskmaster commands with fiery authority, sacrificing underlings without hesitation to achieve victory. Zealot of Kossuth Zealots of Kossuth are fervent warriors devoted to the Lord of Flames, embodying his creed of purification through fire. They believe that all weakness and impurity must be burned away, whether in the forge or on the battlefield. In battle, their eyes blaze with divine intensity as they wade fearlessly into enemy ranks, their burning weapons leaving trails of ash and molten earth. To fall in combat while serving Kossuth’s will is considered the highest honor, for their spirits are said to merge with the eternal flame of their god.

monsterFluff.name: Azer Keeper of the Flame

monsterFluff.name: Ancient Banshee
entries: An ancient banshee is the warped remnant of a once powerful spellcaster who held significant influence such as a queen, royal advisor, or master wizard. Their downfall stemmed from personal failings that include envy, arrogance, or ruthless ambition. In undeath, they retain fragments of their former intellect but are consumed by spite and sorrow. These spirits harbor deep grudges and hunt the descendants of those who betrayed or overshadowed them. Their retained arcane knowledge enhances their banshee powers, allowing them to unleash powerful spells alongside their wails. Any who disturb their resting place or cross their bloodlines face a relentless and vengeful foe.

A Greater and Ancient Banshee's Lair A greater or ancient banshee lingers near the place of her death, usually a site that was once grand or meaningful, such as a castle, keep, temple, or tower. She is bound to this location and cannot stray more than five miles from its broken halls. Within her main lair, every door is sealed with an Arcane Lock that bars the living from entering uninvited. The region containing an ancient or greater banshee’s lair is changed by its foul presence, creating the following effects: Keening Wind. A constant, sorrowful wailing carries on the wind within 1 mile of the lair. Beasts refuse to willingly enter this territory. Additionally, anyone who can hear the wailing has Disadvantage on saving throws made against having the Frightened condition, as well as Constitution saving throws to maintain Concentration. Withering Chill. The temperature within 1 mile of the lair drops unnaturally low, and patches of frost cling to stones, walls, and vegetation even in warm climates. Living creatures do not gain any benefit from Long Rests unless they have resistance or immunity to cold. If the banshee is destroyed or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monsterFluff.name: Greater Basilisk
entries: A greater basilisk, also known as a true basilisk, is an immense, 30-foot reptilian monstrosity that eclipses its lesser kin in both size and ferocity. Its hide resembles rough-hewn stone veined with sickly blue hues, and a ridge of dark crystalline spines crowns its back. Its eyes burn with a baleful light capable of turning flesh to stone in moments, while its heavy claws and fanged maw leave little doubt of its predatory nature. Unlike the common basilisk, it displays a disturbing intelligence, often stalking prey with patience rather than relying solely on ambush. The greater basilisk dwells among caverns, badlands, and shattered ruins, where it uses the rocky terrain to blend seamlessly with its surroundings. Its lairs are littered with half-petrified remains, and the air carries the faint tang of poison and dust. In battle, it combines its petrifying gaze with savage claws and a poisonous breath that saps vitality and dries the flesh of those who inhale it, leaving them weakened and sluggish. Victims sickened by the toxin are far more susceptible to its deadly gaze, and those already turning to stone are often enveloped in its breath to hasten their complete petrification. Though native to the Elemental Plane of Earth, greater basilisks are known to stalk the rugged peaks of the Nether Mountains or claiming the deep subterranean networks of the Galena Mountains. Adventurers might also stumble upon their horrifying, statue-filled lairs hidden amidst the jagged ravines of the Giant's Run Mountains.

monsterFluff.name: Ancient Behir
entries: Very few of these many-legged predators survive long enough to reach such an advanced age, but those that do become truly apocalyptic threats. As apex hunters of the mountains and deep caverns, they are fiercely territorial and harbor an intense, eternal hatred for all dragonkind. Capable of obliterating entire frontier settlements, these solitary terrors demand absolute dominance over their sprawling domains. Stretching nearly a hundred feet in length, their serpentine bodies are protected by overlapping scales that have hardened into impenetrable plates of glittering blue stone. They slither across uneven cliffsides with startling grace to ambush intruders, quickly coiling around prey to squeeze the life from them before swallowing the crushed remains whole. When facing significant resistance, they exhale a devastating, crackling bolt of pure electrical energy that

monsterFluff.name: Young Behir
entries: Freshly hatched from hidden clutches within the craggy peaks of the Sunset Mountains, these elongated reptiles rapidly develop into ruthless carnivores. Their sleek, azure-scaled forms allow them to traverse treacherous ravines and rough crevices with remarkable dexterity. Driven by an innate instinct to claim territory, these juvenile

monsterFluff.name: Elder Orb Beholder
entries: Elder Orb An elder orb is an ancient and highly evolved beholder that has survived far beyond the normal lifespan of its kin. Its spherical body often appears gaunt and sunken, with parchment-like flesh stretched tight over its frame and eye stalks that twist, thin, or atrophy with age. Unlike a standard beholder, an elder orb may bear arcane sigils, crystalline deposits, or warped mutations that testify to centuries of magical experimentation and exposure. Elder orbs are colder, more calculating, and far more patient than typical beholders, weaving long-term schemes rather than lashing out with impulsive destruction. They regard lesser beholders as tools to be used or discarded, dominating them through superior intellect, magic, and manipulation. Only Ultimate Tyrants inspire genuine fear in them, and elder orbs avoid these greater beings entirely, knowing they cannot bend them to their will. A typical elder orb has lost the use of two to four eye stalks due to age and its inability to regenerate them, leaving it with only six to eight functioning eye rays chosen randomly or at the DM’s discretion. These remaining rays are identical to those of a standard beholder but far more potent, enhanced by the creature’s refined natural magic. Beholders To compensate for its lost eyestalks, an elder orb can cast powerful arcane spells rivaling those of accomplished wizards, and it can redirect its antimagic cone to disrupt, nullify, or delay spells cast by others. In the Forgotten Realms, elder orbs are whispered about in Underdark legend, with some believed to rule covert networks beneath Skullport, Menzoberranzan, and the Deep Wastes. Persistent rumors claim that the current Xanathar may secretly be an elder orb hiding its true nature while manipulating events from the shadows. Adventurers might encounter one while exploring ancient beholder hives, dismantling secret cults, or unraveling plots that have simmered for decades under the careful watch of these hidden tyrants. Elder Orb Lairs An elder orb’s lair is usually hidden deep within the Underdark or inside an ancient, maze-like cavern network where intruders can be easily watched, manipulated, or ambushed. It chooses such locations to ensure total control over its territory and to support the elaborate schemes, servants, and magical experiments that sustain its long-term plans. The regional effects of an elder orb are identical to that of a beholder, but has a DC of 14.

monsterFluff.name: Eye of Flame Beholder
entries: Elder Orb An elder orb is an ancient and highly evolved beholder that has survived far beyond the normal lifespan of its kin. Its spherical body often appears gaunt and sunken, with parchment-like flesh stretched tight over its frame and eye stalks that twist, thin, or atrophy with age. Unlike a standard beholder, an elder orb may bear arcane sigils, crystalline deposits, or warped mutations that testify to centuries of magical experimentation and exposure. monsterFluff.name: Ultimate Tyrant Beholder
entries: An ancient blight is a massive, corrupted treant transformed by Gulthias’s blood or by vile rituals performed by druids of Talona. From a distance it resembles a towering, lifeless tree nearly forty feet tall, but its rubbery roots and thorn-covered branches writhe to life when prey draws close. Its bark is blackened and split, its trunk hollowed into a gaping maw lined with rows of spear-like teeth that mark it as something far more monstrous than a common tree blight. Unlike standard tree blights that act on instinct, an ancient blight is driven by malevolent purpose and a cruel, predatory intelligence. It stalks its territory with patience, choosing ambush sites and manipulating its surroundings to funnel prey toward its reach. When engaged, it shows a disturbing awareness of tactics, using its long tendrils to isolate victims and prevent escape. Far stronger and more resilient than lesser blights, an ancient blight possesses immense physical power and can withstand tremendous punishment before falling. Its branches and tendrils bludgeon and constrict with crushing force, capable of smashing structures and grinding armored foes down over long, agonizing moments. Ancient blights typically take root within the most deeply corrupted woodlands, dominating the mutated canopy of the Dire Wood where dark magic saturates the soil. Cultists of Talona also cultivate them deep inside the Chondalwood to violently repel unwanted trespassers. Furthermore, travelers navigating the eastern reaches of the Neverwinter Wood must constantly beware these overgrown predators disguised among the rotting timber.

monsterFluff.name: Bugbear Bruiser
entries: Bugbears Bruiser Bugbear bruisers serve as brutal shock troops who lead charges and shatter defenses with overwhelming strength and expert skill in morningstars and javelins. They strike vital points with crushing precision, inflicting extraordinary pain. Champion of Hruggek This giant bugbear is revered as Hruggek’s chosen and stands as the tribe’s mightiest warrior. Its massive morningstar can stagger even the toughest foes, and it projects Hruggek’s dread presence to sap enemy resolve and leave them exposed to its crushing blows. Footpad Footpads are silent scouts and killers who excel at infiltration. They slip into camps, garrote targets quietly, and vanish without trace. Marauder Marauders are swift killers who disrupt formations and create openings. They flank enemies or rush vulnerable foes to break defensive lines. War Priest of Hruggek War Priests hold great authority, serving as the voice of Hruggek and driving the clan’s ambitions forward. Though they bolster their allies with divine magic, they revel in battle, unleashing Hruggek’s wrath to smite foes and strike fear into the hearts of their enemies.

monsterFluff.name: Greater Bulette
entries: The greater bulette is a rare and terrifying evolution of the land shark that eclipses its lesser kin in both sheer scale and destructive capability. These hulking predators operate as unstoppable forces of nature that can effortlessly reduce an entire thriving village to rubble in mere hours. Boasting the mass of a two-story building, this heavily armored juggernaut tunnels relentlessly through solid stone to satisfy its constant craving for fresh meat. Explorers occasionally stumble upon silent subterranean ruins entirely stripped of life, finding nothing but cavernous boreholes left behind by the creature's feeding frenzy. When hunting, the behemoth prefers to launch a devastating ambush by erupting violently from the earth to send its startled victims flying. Once above ground, it executes a crushing leap to flatten grouped adversaries before aggressively snapping up any remaining survivors to swallow them whole. Due to their immense dietary needs, these solitary titans continuously migrate across vast territories to locate dense hunting grounds. Unfortunate travelers might encounter these horrific burrowers beneath the rugged foothills of the Starspire Mountains, within the desolate badlands of the Stonelands, or tearing through the deep caverns of the Underdark beneath the Dessarin Valley.

monsterFluff.name: Bullywug Brute
entries: Bullywugs Brute Bullywug brutes are towering, seven-foot amphibians resembling thick-muscled humanoid bullfrogs, their bodies built for raw strength and endurance in the swamp. They are the most physically imposing members of their clan and often take on the heaviest tasks, from hauling prey to defending territory. Driven by instinctive hunger and competition, brutes will consume nearly anything that fits into their wide maws, including unfortunate rivals that challenge their place in the hierarchy. Chieftain A bullywug chieftain rises to leadership through quick thinking, resourcefulness, and the ability to rally others during crises. Though it values personal comfort and plentiful food, its decisions are guided by what it believes will keep the clan strong and secure. In battle, the chieftain uses its forceful croaks to coordinate warriors and confuse foes, all while wielding a venom-tipped spear to deter threats and protect its community. Swampstalker Swampstalkers are the clan’s most skilled scouts, perfectly adapted to moving silently through reeds, bogs, and murky shallows. They rely on patience and sharp senses rather than aggression, watching intruders carefully before deciding whether to strike or withdraw. When caution outweighs risk, they slip back to their clan to report what they have observed, helping their kin adapt to dangers in the shifting swamp. Witch Doctor Bullywug witch doctors maintain the clan’s spiritual traditions, drawing upon lingering echoes of ancient magic once linked to the long-dormant deity Ramenos. Their rituals harness natural toxins and strange swamp energies, imbuing their bodies with potent but unpredictable effects. In battle, a witch doctor can spit noxious saliva into an opponent’s face, blinding them until the sticky poison is washed away.

monsterFluff.name: Cambion Hell Knight
entries: Cambions Hell Knight Warlock Trickster Born from the unholy union of mortals and fiends, cambions exhibit a diverse array of specialized lethal talents dictated by their dark lineages. Across Faerûn, tavern whispers speak of these uniquely gifted half-fiends operating from the shadows to enact the devastating will of their abyssal or infernal patrons. Current rumors warn of a silver-tongued cambion trickster blackmailing Masked Lords in Waterdeep, a heavily armored cambion knight rallying orc tribes near the Nether Mountains, and a fiendish cambion warlock unearthing lost Netherese artifacts within the High Moor. Hell Knight Cambion hell knights are brutal half-devil champions shaped into living engines of war, towering over most mortals and clad in jagged infernal plate. Massive horns rise from their helmed visages, and their rigid discipline is matched only by their cruelty and unwavering devotion to the devil legions they command. Their ambitions revolve around conquest, subjugation, and the imposition of infernal order, making them dreaded commanders wherever Hell’s armies march. A cambion hell knight wields an immense hellfire greatsword that chars flesh and curses victims with momentary vulnerability to its magic. It blends overwhelming physical power with an aura of fear that can break the resolve of even hardened warriors. A hell knight charges directly into enemies, blending brute force with terrifying magic to break and scatter opposing lines. Trickster Tricksters are silver-tongued, cunning cambions who excel in deception, charm, and manipulation, delighting in mentally unravelling their prey. Their lithe forms, quick steps, and predatory smiles hide a calculating mind that plots several moves ahead while feigning playful mischief. These cambions operate as arcane tricksters, blending charm and agility to undermine foes before striking with precision. In battle, a Trickster uses rapiers with expert finesse, creating openings through feints, illusions, and sly enchantments. They rely on misdirection and magical trickery, often turning allies against one another or disappearing before retaliation comes. The cambion trickster’s tactics revolve around exploiting confusion, striking from unexpected angles, and ensuring the enemy never knows where the next blow will land. Warlock of the Fiend Cambion warlocks are power-hungry cambions who forge pacts with the very fiends whose blood runs through their veins, allowing them to tap fully into their infernal heritage. Driven by ambition and a boundless hunger for arcane secrets, they often act as advisors, manipulators, or lone agents furthering their own sinister designs. Their fiery eyes, infernal sigils, and commanding presence reveal the forbidden power they wield with absolute confidence. In combat, a cambion warlock hurls searing fire spells and infernal blasts that ravage enemies long before they can close in. They rely on mobility and calculated spell selection, eliminating key threats with banishment, dominate person, or finger of death, before scattering groups with fireballs and walls of fire. A cambion warlock rarely stands alone, calling upon fiendish servants or leveraging their dark pacts to secure overwhelming advantage.
